name: Thymosin Beta-4
subtitle: 43-Amino Acid Regenerative Peptide | Tissue Repair & Healing

molecular.halfLife: Dose-dependent; relatively short (exact values not established)

overview: Naturally occurring 43-amino acid peptide crucial for tissue repair, wound healing, and cellular regeneration. Promotes angiogenesis, reduces inflammation, and supports cell migration and differentiation.
mechanism: Direct systemic delivery provides optimal bioavailability and distribution to target tissues for comprehensive regenerative effects including angiogenesis, cell migration, and differentiation.

items.name: Wound Healing Acceleration
items.description: Clinical trials show 67% complete healing versus 25% placebo in pressure ulcers.

items.name: Post-Surgical Recovery
items.description: Enhanced tissue regeneration and reduced healing time in surgical wounds.

items.name: Soft Tissue Injury Repair
items.description: Accelerated recovery in muscle, tendon, and ligament injuries.

items.name: Cardiac Protection Post-MI
items.description: Phase 2b trials show 43% reduction in infarct size when given within 6 hours.

items.name: Stroke Recovery Enhancement
items.description: Improved neurological outcomes and functional recovery in animal models.

deliveryMethods.overview: Systemic regenerative effects with proven clinical efficacy and optimal bioavailability.

protocols.route: IV bolus within 6 hours of MI

steps: Allow lyophilized powder to reach room temperature before reconstitution
Add bacteriostatic water slowly to vial wall, avoiding direct powder contact
Gently swirl (do not shake vigorously) until completely dissolved
Inspect solution for clarity—should be clear and colorless
Store reconstituted solution at 2-8°C and use within 14 days
Use proper aseptic technique and rotate injection sites

deliveryMethods.overview: Direct wound application with proven corneal healing and minimal systemic exposure.

protocols.route: Direct topical application to wound bed

interactions.notes: TB-500 is the bioactive fragment of Thymosin β4. Combined use provides full-length peptide benefits plus concentrated bioactive effects.
Complementary healing mechanisms—Tβ4 promotes cell migration and angiogenesis while BPC-157 enhances gastric protection.
No direct interactions reported. Both promote tissue repair through different pathways.
Complementary growth factor effects without direct interaction.
Steroids may reduce effectiveness by inhibiting inflammatory responses necessary for optimal healing.
Tβ4 promotes angiogenesis which may affect bleeding risk. Monitor coagulation parameters.
Theoretical concern about promoting angiogenesis in cancer patients. Avoid during active chemotherapy.

timeline.effects: Initial tissue response, possible mild injection site reactions
Accelerated healing becomes apparent, reduced inflammation
Significant tissue regeneration and functional improvement
Sustained benefits and near-complete healing in responsive conditions

stopSigns: Severe injection site reactions or persistent inflammation
Signs of systemic allergic reaction (rash, breathing difficulty)
Any signs of infection at injection sites
monitoring: Monitor injection sites for local reactions
Use sterile technique to prevent infection
Start with lower doses to assess tolerance
Monitor coagulation parameters if on anticoagulants

good: Proper sterile packaging with intact seals
Appropriate molecular weight confirmed via HPLC/MS
warning: Room temperature storage claims—legitimate product requires refrigeration after reconstitution
bad: Cloudy or discolored solution indicating degradation
Crystallization after reconstitution suggesting improper storage

references.title: Phase II Dry Eye Clinical Trial
references.keyFindings: 72 subjects showing significant positive effects on ocular discomfort and corneal staining with excellent safety profile.

references.title: Phase I Human Safety Study: Intravenous Thymosin β4
references.keyFindings: First-in-human study establishing safety and pharmacokinetics in healthy volunteers with no dose-limiting toxicity.

references.title: Cardiac Protection Pilot Study: Thymosin β4 in STEMI Patients
references.keyFindings: First human study in heart attack patients demonstrating potential clinical benefits in repairing damaged tissue.

references.title: Neurotrophic Keratopathy Compassionate Use Trial
references.keyFindings: Complete clearing of persistent corneal defects in most subjects using topical formulation.

quickStats.typicalDose: 2-6mg per injection (varies by protocol and severity)
quickStats.frequency: Daily for acute healing, 2x weekly for maintenance/chronic conditions
